title: stevia berlandieri
intro: Stevia berlandieri, also known as the wild sugar bush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, particularly stevioside and rebaudioside, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Stevia berlandieri is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, lowering blood sugar levels, and exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal and health-promoting effects.
